name: NVMe PCIe Expansion Card
description: PCIe add-in card with 16× M.2 NVMe slots
raidNote: No onboard RAID — use software mdadm RAID10/RAID5
bifurcationNote: Card has its own PCIe switch, no motherboard bifurcation needed
thermalNote: 80-120W heat load in a PCIe slot. Chassis not designed for it — expect throttling without fan shroud.
priceNote: ~$2,500-3,500 estimated for a high-density PCIe NVMe card

3.note: Gen3 x16 ceiling — 25% of rated performance

4.note: Gen4 x16 ceiling — 50% of rated performance

caveats: No hot-swap — server may need downtime to replace M.2 drives
PCIe lane contention — consumes a full x16 slot from the server lane budget
Thermal: 80-120W of heat in chassis not designed for it
No local protection on card — use software RAID or higher-level replication
Existing legacy fleet has Gen3 slots — card runs at 25% rated speed
Upgrade bridge, not a long-term architecture
whyRecommended: A lower-cost way to add NVMe capacity to an existing server, with caveats around hot-swap, lane limits, thermal design, and supportability.
